name: map-reduce-refactor
description: Planner partitions the refactor into independent batches written to .agent-loop/batches.json, then a serial loop has one Writer process one batch per iteration, with a Judge gate after each. Terminates when batches.done == true.

nodes.user:
Partition the following refactor into 3–15 independent batches. Each batch is a JSON object with {id, title, files: [...], acceptance: [...] }. Write them to .agent-loop/batches.json in the shape {"batches": [...], "done": false}. Then write a human-readable summary to .agent-loop/spec.md. Do NOT write code in this turn.

User request: {{input.prompt}}

subgraph.user: Round {{loop.round}}. Read .agent-loop/batches.json. Pick the first batch whose `done` is not true. Implement it end-to-end (typecheck + tests must pass for touched files). When done, set that batch's `done: true`; if ALL batches are done, set the top-level `done: true`. Commit everything (code + .agent-loop bookkeeping) in a single commit. Also append a paragraph to .agent-loop/progress.txt and, ONLY if top-level `done: true`, append the sentinel `<promise>COMPLETE</promise>` as the last line.
Round {{loop.round}}. Audit the batch the Worker just completed. Run the acceptance steps for that batch only. Write .agent-loop/judge-{{loop.round}}.md ending with VERDICT: PASS or VERDICT: FAIL.
